Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze a provided event log of a Procure to Pay P2P process identify anomalies deviations from standard best practices and explain why they are problematic based on general business knowledge

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Standard P2P Process What is the normal flow Need Identification Create Purchase Requisition PR Approval Approve Purchase Requisition Ordering Issue Purchase Order PO Receipt Receive Goods Invoicing Vendor Invoice Received Verification Match Invoice to PO GRN Three Way Match Payment Release Payment Closure Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Key Controls Approval must happen before ordering PO must exist before goods are received ideally and definitely before payment Goods receipt usually happens before or around the same time as invoicing but payment should happen after verification Three way matching PO Goods Receipt Invoice is crucial for fraud prevention and accuracy Payment should not happen before the invoice is received and matched Payment should not happen before goods are received unless it is a specific pre payment scenario but standard P2P implies goods services first

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 PR Created PR Approved PO Issued Goods Received Invoice Received Match Payment Close Timeline 2 1 to 2 7 Flow Logical Approval before PO PO before Goods Invoice after Goods Match before Payment Verdict Normal

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 PR Created PO Issued Invoice Received Payment Released PR Approved Goods Received Match Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies PO before Approval PO issued at 08 40 PR Approved at 08 00 next day This is a major control failure You cannot order before approval Payment before Invoice Match Payment released at 07 15 Match happened at 08 00 next day Paying before verifying the invoice against the PO is risky Payment before Goods Receipt Payment released Feb 3 Goods received Feb 4 Paying before receiving goods is high risk Invoice before Goods Invoice received before goods received though sometimes okay usually goods come first or simultaneously Approval after PO As noted in point 1

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Verdict Highly Anomalous Problematic

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 PR Created PR Approved PO Issued Payment Released Invoice Received Goods Received Match Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies Payment before Invoice Payment released Feb 8 Invoice received Feb 9 How do you pay without an invoice This suggests a blind payment or potential fraud Payment before Goods Payment released Feb 8 Goods received Feb 10 Paying before receiving goods is risky Payment before Matching Payment released Feb 8 Match happened Feb 11 Payment was made without verification

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Verdict Highly Anomalous Problematic

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 PR Created PR Approved Goods Received PO Issued Invoice Match Payment Close

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomalies Goods before PO Goods received at 09 00 PO issued at 09 15 Receiving goods before a Purchase Order exists is a control violation maverick buying It makes matching difficult later The rest of the flow Invoice Match Payment seems okay relative to each other but the root cause Goods before PO is the issue

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Verdict Anomalous Problematic

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Synthesize and Structure the Output Start with a brief introduction acknowledging the standard P2P flow Go through each Case 1001 1004 For the Normal case 1001 briefly mention it serves as a baseline For the Anomalous cases 1002 1003 1004 list specific violations For each violation explain the Risk Problem Fraud Financial Loss Compliance Operational Efficiency Conclude with a summary of the types of risks identified Segregation of Duties Control Failures

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Refine the Analysis Self Correction Double Check Check Case 1002 again PR Approved on Feb 3 at 08 00 PO Issued Feb 1 at 08 40 Yes definitely PO before Approval Payment Feb 3 07 15 Match Feb 5 08 00 Payment before Match Goods Feb 4 Payment before Goods

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check Case 1003 again Payment Feb 8 Invoice Feb 9 Goods Feb 10 This is Payment in Advance without documentation Very suspicious

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check Case 1004 again Goods Feb 6 09 00 PO Feb 6 09 15 This is Receipt before Order Often called Maverick Spend Terminology Use terms like Three Way Match Maverick Buying Segregation of Duties Control Environment

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Drafting the Response Mental Outline Intro Define standard flow Case 1001 Green light Case 1002 Red light PO before Approval Payment before Match Goods Case 1003 Red light Payment before Invoice Goods Match Case 1004 Orange Red light Goods before PO Summary Categorize risks Fraud Compliance Operational

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Polish Ensure clear formatting bullet points bold text for readability Ensure the explanation clearly links the anomaly to the business risk for example Why is Payment before Invoice bad Risk of overpayment or fraud

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review against Prompt Did I identify anomalies Yes Did I explain why they are problematic Yes Did I rely on general P2P knowledge Yes
